name: Tardigrade

visual_attributes.morphology: short, plump body with four pairs of hollow unjointed legs
distinctive_features: eight legs ending in claws or sticky pads
cuticle covering with sclerotised proteins and chitin
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 0.05 to 0.5 mm in length, up to 1.3 mm for largest species
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: body cavity is a haemocoel with open circulatory system

domain_specific_visual.LEGS: four pairs, each ending in claws or pads

secondary_functions: feeding on plant or animal cell fluids

domain_specific_functional.GAS_EXCHANGE: diffusion through cuticle and body cavity
domain_specific_functional.LOCOMOTION: antagonistic muscle pairs for leg movement

relationships.description: Tardigrades are preyed upon by mites, spiders, and beetle larvae.
Tardigrades consume nematodes and other small organisms.
Tardigrades host various microbial symbionts in their microbiomes.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: cosmopolitan, including mountaintops, deep sea, rainforests, and Antarctic
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of resilience and survival in extreme conditions

contrastive_constraints: NOT visible to the naked eye
